response.justification: The candidate is a capable full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and DLang experience, but the role explicitly requires 8 years of C++ programming and the resume shows only roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis. There is also no demonstrated experience building or leading a cloud compute platform, and no formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers. The mismatch on the hard minimum qualifications makes this a clear pass.
strengths: Distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second
Long track record of senior engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infra
Strong engineering-practice background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring via workshops
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum — only a brief NI stint and a thesis project
No cloud compute platform or production cloud architecture experience listed
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / production cloud architecture experience
Distributed systems and high-throughput infrastructure background
